article_title: JetBrains Unveils Central, a Control Plane for Agentic Development, as IntelliJ 2026.1 Opens the IDE to Cursor, Codex, and Any ACP-Compatible Agent

content_preview.title: JetBrains Unveils Central, a Control Plane for Agentic Development, as IntelliJ 2026.1 Opens the IDE to Cursor, Codex, and Any ACP-Compatible Agent
content_preview.summary: JetBrains launches Central, a governance and execution platform for AI coding agents, alongside IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 with Agent Client Protocol support and the Air agentic IDE in public preview, signaling a decisive pivot toward agent-driven development.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

JetBrains shipped three major announcements in March that together redraw the boundaries of what an IDE is expected to do. IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 arrived with native support for external AI agents through the Agent Client Protocol. Air, an agentic IDE built on the abandoned Fleet project, entered public preview. And on March 24, JetBrains introduced [Central](https://blog.jetbrains.com/blog/2026/03/24/introducing-jetbrains-central-an-open-system-for-agentic-software-development/), a g...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent developer tools coverage synthesizing three related JetBrains announcements coherently.
editor_notes.source_verification: JetBrains Central blog (fetched): Confirms March 24 launch, governance/execution/optimization layers, Q2 2026 EAP, 22% agent usage survey. JetBrains AI blog (fetched): Confirms Cursor ACP integration, agent picker, no subscription needed. The Register and IntelliJ blog also cited.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Survey data (22% agents, 90% AI usage) confirmed from JetBrains Central blog.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Good analytical framing of industry shift.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong developer tools news. Ready for publication.
